states.name: Abia
states.summary: Abia is a state in the Southeastern region of Nigeria. The state's capital is Umuahia and its most populous city is Aba. The legislature is the Abia State House of Assembly. Abia is bordered to the west by Imo, east by Cross River, south by Rivers, northwest by Anambra, and northeast by Enugu and Ebonyi. The state is known for its commercial activities, particularly in Aba, which is a major industrial and commercial hub famous for its leather goods and textiles.
states.economy: Abia's economy is driven by commerce, manufacturing, and oil production. Aba is renowned for its artisans who produce shoes, bags, clothing, and other leather goods. The state also has significant crude oil deposits.
notable_facts: Aba is one of the most commercially active cities in Nigeria
Home to the Ariaria International Market, one of the largest markets in West Africa
Known for its skilled craftsmen and 'Aba Made' brand of locally produced goods

states.name: Adamawa
states.summary: Adamawa is a state in northeastern Nigeria, named after the Fulani leader Modibbo Adama. Its capital and largest city is Yola, also known as Jimeta. The state shares an international border with Cameroon to the east. Adamawa is known for its diverse ethnic groups, beautiful landscape including the Mandara Mountains, and the Benue and Gongola rivers.
states.economy: Adamawa's economy is primarily agrarian. Major crops include yam, millet, sorghum, rice, cotton, and groundnuts. Cattle rearing is also prominent. The state has untapped mineral resources including limestone, gypsum, and granite.
notable_facts: Shares an international border with Cameroon
Home to the Mandara Mountains
Named after the Fulani leader Modibbo Adama

states.name: Akwa Ibom
states.legislature: Akwa Ibom State House of Assembly
states.summary: Akwa Ibom is a state in the South South geopolitical zone of Nigeria. It is Nigeria's highest crude oil producing state and one of the most revenue-generating states in the country. The state capital is Uyo. Akwa Ibom is bordered by Cross River to the east, Rivers and Abia to the west, and the Atlantic Ocean to the south. It is known for its rich cultural heritage, hospitality, and the Ibom people.
states.economy: Akwa Ibom is the largest oil-producing state in Nigeria. Other economic activities include fishing, agriculture (palm oil, cocoa, cassava), and commerce. The Ibom Power Plant is one of the largest in the country.
notable_facts: Largest oil-producing state in Nigeria
Home to Ibom Air, a state-owned airline
Known for the Calabar-Itu Road Bridge and Uyo City

states.name: Anambra
states.summary: Anambra is a state in southeastern Nigeria. Its capital is Awka, while Onitsha is its largest and most commercially significant city. Anambra is one of Nigeria's smallest states by land area but among the most densely populated. The state is known for its entrepreneurial spirit, the famous Onitsha Main Market — one of the largest markets in Africa — and its rich Igbo cultural heritage.
states.economy: Anambra's economy is highly commercial, driven by trade at Onitsha, manufacturing, and services. The state also has agricultural activities including yam, cassava, and palm oil production.
notable_facts: Home to Onitsha Main Market, one of the largest markets in Africa
One of the most densely populated states in Nigeria
Named after the Anambra River

states.name: Bauchi
states.summary: Bauchi State is located in the northeastern part of Nigeria. Its capital is Bauchi city. The state is known for its cultural diversity with over 55 ethnic groups and languages, and for the famous Yankari National Park — Nigeria's most popular game reserve. Bauchi borders seven states, making it one of the most centrally located states in northern Nigeria.
states.economy: Bauchi's economy is driven by agriculture, trade, and tourism. Major crops include groundnuts, cotton, sorghum, millet, and rice. The state has significant mineral resources including gold, tin, and columbite.
notable_facts: Home to Yankari National Park, Nigeria's premier wildlife reserve
One of the largest states by area in Nigeria
Has over 55 ethnic groups and languages

states.name: Bayelsa
states.summary: Bayelsa State is located in the Niger Delta region of southern Nigeria. Its capital is Yenagoa. The state is predominantly made up of creeks, rivers, and estuaries and is one of Nigeria's major oil-producing states. Bayelsa is the smallest state in southern Nigeria and is home to the Ijaw people, the fourth largest ethnic group in Nigeria.
states.economy: Oil and gas dominate Bayelsa's economy. The state has vast petroleum reserves and hosts many oil company facilities. Fishing and farming (yam, cassava, plantain) are also important.
notable_facts: Smallest state in southern Nigeria
Predominantly inhabited by the Ijaw people
Major oil-producing state in the Niger Delta

states.name: Benue
states.summary: Benue State is located in north-central Nigeria and is nicknamed 'The Food Basket of the Nation' due to its highly productive agricultural land. Its capital is Makurdi, situated on the banks of the Benue River. The state is home to the Tiv, Idoma, and Igede peoples. Benue is known for its role in feeding the nation with yam, sorghum, cassava, and other food crops.
states.economy: Agriculture is the backbone of Benue's economy. The state is one of Nigeria's largest producers of yam, cassava, sorghum, soybeans, and beniseed. The Benue River provides extensive fishing opportunities.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Food Basket of the Nation'
Home to the Tiv, Nigeria's fourth-largest ethnic group
The Benue River runs through the state

states.name: Borno
states.summary: Borno is the largest state in Nigeria by land area, located in the far northeast of the country. Its capital is Maiduguri. The state borders three countries: Niger, Chad, and Cameroon. Borno is historically significant as the seat of the ancient Kanem-Bornu Empire, one of the longest-lasting empires in African history. Lake Chad, a major natural resource, borders the state.
states.economy: Borno's economy is primarily agricultural, with fishing in Lake Chad being a major activity. Crops include millet, sorghum, wheat, and groundnuts. Trade with neighboring countries is also significant.
notable_facts: Largest state in Nigeria by land area
Historical seat of the ancient Kanem-Bornu Empire
Borders three countries: Niger, Chad, and Cameroon

states.name: Cross River
states.legislature: Cross River State House of Assembly
states.summary: Cross River State is located in the South South geopolitical zone of Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Calabar, one of the most tourism-friendly cities in Nigeria. The state borders Cameroon to the east and has significant forest reserves. Cross River is famous for its vibrant Calabar Carnival — dubbed Africa's biggest street party — and its diverse wildlife including gorillas in the Cross River National Park.
states.economy: Cross River's economy is based on agriculture, tourism, and trade. The state produces cocoa, palm oil, timber, and rubber. Tourism is growing, driven by the Calabar Carnival and ecotourism.
notable_facts: Home to the Calabar Carnival, Africa's biggest street party
Cross River National Park hosts endangered Cross River gorillas
Calabar was the first capital of colonial Nigeria

states.name: Delta
states.summary: Delta State is located in the South South geopolitical zone of Nigeria. Its capital is Asaba while Warri is its largest and most commercially active city. Delta State is a major oil-producing state and one of the most economically significant states in Nigeria. The state is home to diverse ethnic groups including the Urhobo, Itsekiri, Ijaw, Isoko, and Anioma people.
states.economy: Oil and gas are the dominant economic activities in Delta State. The state is also significant for agriculture (yam, cassava, rubber, palm oil) and commerce, especially in Warri which is a major oil-industry hub.
notable_facts: One of Nigeria's top oil-producing states
Warri is a major hub for Nigeria's oil industry
Home to diverse ethnic groups with rich cultural traditions

states.name: Ebonyi
states.summary: Ebonyi State is located in southeastern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Abakaliki. The state was created in 1996 from parts of Enugu and Cross River states. Ebonyi is known for its salt production and rice cultivation. The state is predominantly rural with the Igbo people making up the majority of the population.
states.economy: Ebonyi's economy is largely agricultural. The state is one of the largest rice producers in Nigeria. Other crops include yam, cassava, and potatoes. Salt mining around Abakaliki is also an important economic activity.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Salt Lake State' due to salt deposits around Abakaliki
One of the newest states in Nigeria, created in 1996
Major rice-producing state in Nigeria

states.name: Edo
states.summary: Edo State is located in the South South geopolitical zone of Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Benin City, which was the seat of the ancient and powerful Benin Kingdom — one of the oldest and most developed states in West Africa. Benin City is renowned for its ancient art, including the famous Benin Bronzes. The state is home to diverse ethnic groups, predominantly the Edo people.
states.economy: Edo's economy is driven by oil production, rubber farming, timber, and commerce. Benin City is a major commercial center. The state has significant rubber plantations, making it one of the largest rubber producers in Nigeria.
notable_facts: Home to the ancient Benin Kingdom and its world-famous bronze art
The Benin Bronzes are displayed in museums worldwide
Benin City was one of the first cities in the world to have street lighting

states.name: Ekiti
states.summary: Ekiti State is located in southwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Ado-Ekiti. The state was created in 1996 from the old Ondo State. Ekiti is known as 'The Fountain of Knowledge' due to its high literacy rate and the large number of professors and educated elites it has produced. The state has a rich Yoruba cultural heritage.
states.economy: Ekiti's economy is based on agriculture (cocoa, yam, cassava, timber) and civil service. The state has potential in solid mineral mining including granite, quartz, and kaolin.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Fountain of Knowledge' for its high literacy rate
Produces the highest number of professors per capita in Nigeria
Rich in Yoruba cultural traditions and festivals

states.name: Enugu
states.summary: Enugu State is located in southeastern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Enugu, historically known as 'The Coal City' due to its significant coal deposits discovered in 1909. Enugu was the capital of the former Eastern Region and served as the capital of the short-lived Republic of Biafra during the Nigerian Civil War (1967–1970). The state is predominantly Igbo.
states.economy: Enugu's economy is driven by coal mining (historically), trade, agriculture, and services. The state produces yam, rice, and cassava. Enugu city is a growing commercial and educational hub.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Coal City' — coal was discovered here in 1909
Was capital of the Republic of Biafra during the Civil War
Home to several major universities and research institutions

states.name: Federal Capital Territory
states.summary: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) is the capital territory of Nigeria and home to Abuja, Nigeria's capital city. Abuja replaced Lagos as the nation's capital on 12 December 1991. It is a planned city situated in the center of the country to provide a geographically neutral capital. Abuja is the political and administrative center of Nigeria and hosts all three arms of government.
states.economy: Abuja's economy is driven by government and public administration, real estate, construction, and services. It is one of the fastest-growing cities in Africa and has a booming hospitality and retail sector.
notable_facts: Nigeria's capital city and seat of the federal government
One of the few purpose-built capital cities in Africa
Home to Aso Rock, the presidential villa

states.name: Gombe
states.summary: Gombe State is located in northeastern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Gombe. The state was created in 1996 from part of Bauchi State. Gombe is referred to as 'The Jewel in the Savannah' and is one of the major commercial centers in northeastern Nigeria. The state is ethnically diverse with groups including the Fulani, Tera, Waja, and Tangale.
states.economy: Gombe's economy is largely agricultural. Major crops include groundnuts, cotton, sorghum, millet, and maize. The state has tourism potential with the Gombe-Abuja road being a key access route.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Jewel in the Savannah'
One of the newest states in Nigeria, created in 1996
Diverse ethnic composition with over 20 distinct groups

states.name: Imo
states.summary: Imo State is located in the South East geopolitical zone of Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Owerri. Imo is one of Nigeria's smallest states by land area but is among the most oil-rich. The state is home to the Igbo people and is known for its cultural festivals, arts, and the Oguta Lake — the largest natural lake in southeastern Nigeria.
states.economy: Imo's economy is driven by oil production, agriculture (yam, cassava, palm oil), and commerce. Owerri is a growing service and entertainment hub in the South East.
notable_facts: Home to Oguta Lake, the largest natural lake in southeastern Nigeria
One of Nigeria's significant oil-producing states
Known for cultural festivals and Igbo traditional arts

states.name: Jigawa
states.summary: Jigawa State is located in northwestern Nigeria. Its capital is Dutse. The state was created in 1991 from part of Kano State. Jigawa is predominantly rural and agrarian, with the Hausa-Fulani people making up the majority of the population. The state is known for its cultural heritage, traditional governance structure, and the Hadejia-Nguru Wetlands.
states.economy: Jigawa's economy is primarily agricultural. The state produces groundnuts, millet, sorghum, cotton, and vegetables. The Hadejia-Jama'are River basin supports irrigation farming.
notable_facts: Home to the Hadejia-Nguru Wetlands, an important ecosystem
One of the least urbanized states in Nigeria
Carved out of Kano State in 1991

states.name: Kaduna
states.summary: Kaduna State is located in northwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Kaduna. The state was the administrative capital of northern Nigeria during the colonial era and remains one of the most important commercial and political centers in the north. Kaduna is ethnically and religiously diverse, with a roughly equal split between Christian and Muslim populations.
states.economy: Kaduna's economy is driven by manufacturing, trade, and agriculture. It has one of the largest concentrations of industry in northern Nigeria, including textiles, steel, and petroleum refining. Agriculture produces cotton, groundnuts, maize, and sorghum.
notable_facts: Former administrative capital of northern Nigeria
One of the most industrialized states in northern Nigeria
Ethnically and religiously diverse with roughly equal Christian and Muslim populations

states.name: Kano
states.summary: Kano State is located in northwestern Nigeria. Its capital, Kano city, is the second most populous city in Nigeria after Lagos. Kano has been an important center of Islamic scholarship and commerce for over a millennium. The ancient Kano city walls, Kurmi Market — one of West Africa's oldest markets — and the emir's palace are iconic historical landmarks. The state is predominantly Hausa-Fulani.
states.economy: Kano is Nigeria's largest commercial city in the north. Its economy is driven by manufacturing (textiles, leather, food processing), trade, and agriculture. The Kano River Irrigation Project supports large-scale farming.
notable_facts: Second most populous city in Nigeria
One of the oldest cities in West Africa with over 1,000 years of history
Kurmi Market is one of West Africa's oldest and largest markets

states.name: Katsina
states.summary: Katsina State is located in northwestern Nigeria, bordering the Republic of Niger to the north. Its capital and largest city is Katsina. The state was historically the seat of the powerful Katsina Emirate and was one of the original Hausa Bakwai city-states. Katsina is the birthplace of President Muhammadu Buhari and has a rich history of Islamic scholarship and trade.
states.economy: Katsina's economy is primarily agricultural. The state produces groundnuts, millet, sorghum, and cotton. Livestock rearing is also important. The state has potential in leather production and solid minerals.
notable_facts: Birthplace of former President Muhammadu Buhari
One of the original Hausa Bakwai city-states
Rich history of Islamic scholarship dating back centuries

states.name: Kebbi
states.summary: Kebbi State is located in northwestern Nigeria. Its capital is Birnin Kebbi. The state was created in 1991 from part of Sokoto State. Kebbi is historically associated with the Kebbi Kingdom, which was founded in the 16th century and successfully resisted Sokoto's dominance. The state is known for its fishing culture, particularly the famous Argungu Fishing Festival.
states.economy: Agriculture and fishing are the mainstays of Kebbi's economy. The state is a major rice and fish producer. Rice farming has been significantly boosted through government programs.
notable_facts: Home to the famous Argungu Fishing Festival, a UNESCO-recognized cultural event
One of Nigeria's leading rice-producing states
Historically home to the Kebbi Kingdom that resisted the Sokoto Caliphate

states.name: Kogi
states.summary: Kogi State is located in north-central Nigeria and is often called 'the Confluence State' because Lokoja, the state capital, is located at the confluence of the Niger and Benue rivers — the two largest rivers in Nigeria. Kogi borders the most states of any state in Nigeria. It is ethnically diverse, with the Igala, Ebira, and Okun-Yoruba as the major ethnic groups.
states.economy: Kogi's economy is driven by agriculture, mining, and commerce. The state has significant deposits of iron ore, coal, limestone, and marble. Agriculture produces yam, cassava, and grains.
notable_facts: Located at the confluence of the Niger and Benue rivers
Borders more states than any other state in Nigeria
Lokoja was the first administrative capital of colonial Nigeria

states.name: Kwara
states.summary: Kwara State is located in north-central Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Ilorin. The state is often described as Nigeria's 'middle ground' as it sits at the intersection of the northern and southern cultures. Ilorin, a major Yoruba city, came under the Sokoto Caliphate in the 19th century, giving the city a unique blend of Yoruba and Hausa-Fulani cultures.
states.economy: Kwara's economy is driven by agriculture, trade, and public administration. The state produces yam, cassava, cotton, tobacco, and shea butter. Ilorin is a growing commercial city and home to the University of Ilorin.
notable_facts: Ilorin is a unique blend of Yoruba and Hausa-Fulani cultures
Home to the University of Ilorin, one of Nigeria's leading universities
Known as 'The State of Harmony'

states.name: Lagos
states.summary: Lagos State is the smallest state in Nigeria by land area yet the most populous, with an estimated population exceeding 21 million people. It is the economic and commercial capital of Nigeria and one of the largest cities in Africa. Lagos is a global city and the financial hub of West Africa. The state capital is Ikeja while Lagos Island is the historic commercial center. Lagos was Nigeria's capital city until Abuja was designated in 1991.
states.economy: Lagos accounts for over 25% of Nigeria's GDP. Its economy is the most diversified in the country, driven by financial services, trade, manufacturing, entertainment, technology, and the port. Lagos Port is the busiest in West Africa.
notable_facts: Most populous city in Nigeria and one of the largest in Africa
Accounts for over 25% of Nigeria's total GDP
Was Nigeria's capital until 1991 when Abuja took over

states.name: Nasarawa
states.summary: Nasarawa State is located in north-central Nigeria. Its capital is Lafia. The state was created in 1996 from part of Plateau State. Nasarawa is known as the 'Home of Solid Minerals' due to its extensive mineral deposits. The state is ethnically diverse with over 50 ethnic groups including the Eggon, Mada, Kanuri, and Tiv.
states.economy: Nasarawa has a strong agricultural sector producing yam, cassava, rice, and vegetables. The state has vast mineral resources including baryte, gemstones, columbite, galena, and gold, earning it the nickname 'Home of Solid Minerals'.
notable_facts: Known as 'Home of Solid Minerals' for its extensive mineral deposits
Over 50 distinct ethnic groups
Proximate to Abuja, driving real estate and economic growth

states.name: Niger
states.summary: Niger State is the largest state in Nigeria by land area. Its capital is Minna. The state is located in the north-central zone and is home to some of Nigeria's most important hydroelectric infrastructure, including the Kainji and Shiroro dams on the Niger River. Niger State is ethnically diverse with the Nupe, Gbagyi, Hausa, and Fulani as major groups.
states.economy: Niger State's economy is driven by agriculture, hydroelectric power generation, and solid minerals. The Kainji Dam provides a significant portion of Nigeria's electricity. Major crops include yam, cassava, rice, and sorghum.
notable_facts: Largest state in Nigeria by land area
Home to the Kainji Dam, a major source of Nigeria's electricity
Named after the Niger River which runs through it

states.name: Ogun
states.summary: Ogun State is located in southwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Abeokuta, literally meaning 'under a rock' — named after the Olumo Rock which served as a refuge during 19th century wars. Ogun is known as the 'Gateway State' because it serves as the gateway between Lagos and the rest of Nigeria. The state is the most industrialized outside Lagos and Abuja.
states.economy: Ogun has one of Nigeria's most diversified economies. It is the leading state for manufacturing outside Lagos, with many industrial estates. Agriculture produces cocoa, palm oil, and food crops. The state benefits economically from its proximity to Lagos.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Gateway State'
Abeokuta is named after Olumo Rock, a historic landmark
Most industrialized state outside Lagos and Abuja

states.name: Ondo
states.summary: Ondo State is located in southwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Akure. The state is one of the leading producers of cocoa in Nigeria and is sometimes called 'The Sunshine State.' Ondo has a rich Yoruba cultural heritage and is home to significant natural resources including bitumen deposits and offshore oil fields.
states.economy: Ondo's economy is driven by cocoa farming, oil production (offshore), and bitumen deposits. The state has the second largest bitumen deposit in the world. Agriculture also produces timber, kola nuts, and food crops.
notable_facts: Has the second largest bitumen deposit in the world
One of Nigeria's top cocoa-producing states
Known as 'The Sunshine State'

states.name: Osun
states.summary: Osun State is located in southwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Osogbo. The state is named after the Osun River and is known for its deep Yoruba cultural heritage. The Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is dedicated to Osun, the Yoruba goddess of the river. The state hosts the annual Osun-Osogbo Festival, one of Nigeria's most important cultural events.
states.economy: Osun's economy is based on agriculture (cocoa, yam, cassava, kola nuts), trade, and civil service. The state has growing interest in cultural tourism due to its UNESCO World Heritage Site.
notable_facts: Home to the Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove, a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Hosts the annual Osun-Osogbo Festival, a major cultural event
Named after the Osun River

states.name: Oyo
states.summary: Oyo State is located in southwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Ibadan, the third largest city in Nigeria and once the largest city in sub-Saharan Africa. The state is named after the ancient Oyo Empire, one of West Africa's most powerful pre-colonial states. Oyo is a center of Yoruba culture, learning, and commerce.
states.economy: Oyo's economy is driven by commerce, agriculture, and public services. Ibadan is a major commercial and educational hub. Agriculture produces cocoa, cassava, yam, and timber. The state has several universities including the University of Ibadan, Nigeria's oldest university.
notable_facts: Ibadan was once the largest city in sub-Saharan Africa
Home to the University of Ibadan, Nigeria's oldest university
Named after the historic Oyo Empire, one of West Africa's greatest kingdoms

states.name: Plateau
states.summary: Plateau State is located in north-central Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Jos, situated on the Jos Plateau at an elevation of about 1,200 meters above sea level, giving it a cool, temperate climate unusual for Nigeria. Known as 'The Home of Peace and Tourism,' the state is famous for its beautiful scenery, cool weather, and diverse ethnic groups. Jos is also an important mining town historically known for tin and columbite.
states.economy: Plateau State's economy is driven by mining, agriculture, and tourism. The state was historically Nigeria's major tin-mining region. Agriculture produces vegetables, potatoes, fruits, and grains. The cool climate makes it ideal for horticulture.
notable_facts: Known as 'The Home of Peace and Tourism'
Jos enjoys a cool, temperate climate due to its high elevation
Historically the center of Nigeria's tin and columbite mining

states.name: Rivers
states.summary: Rivers State is located in the South South geopolitical zone of Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Port Harcourt, the oil capital of Nigeria. The state is one of Nigeria's most economically significant, contributing a large share of the nation's oil revenue. Port Harcourt is a major industrial city and the hub of Nigeria's oil and gas industry. Rivers is home to diverse ethnic groups including the Ikwerre, Ogoni, Ijaw, and Kalabari.
states.economy: Rivers State's economy is dominated by oil and gas production. Port Harcourt hosts the headquarters of many international oil companies. Other activities include shipping, trade, and agriculture.
notable_facts: Port Harcourt is Nigeria's oil capital and second busiest port
One of the highest revenue-generating states in Nigeria
Home to a significant population of diverse ethnic minorities

states.name: Sokoto
states.summary: Sokoto State is located in the extreme northwest of Nigeria. Its capital, Sokoto city, is the seat of the Sokoto Sultanate, the most important Islamic institution in Nigeria. Sokoto was the headquarters of the Sokoto Caliphate founded by Usman dan Fodio in the early 19th century — one of the largest empires in African history. The Sultan of Sokoto is regarded as the spiritual leader of Nigerian Muslims.
states.economy: Sokoto's economy is primarily agricultural. Major crops include millet, sorghum, groundnuts, and cotton. The state also has significant deposits of limestone and gypsum used in cement manufacturing.
notable_facts: Seat of the Sokoto Sultanate, the spiritual home of Nigeria's Muslims
Was the center of the Sokoto Caliphate, one of the largest empires in African history
Home to the Usman Dan Fodio University

states.name: Taraba
states.summary: Taraba State is located in northeastern Nigeria. Its capital is Jalingo. The state is nicknamed 'Nature's Gift to the Nation' due to its abundant natural resources and scenic beauty. Taraba is one of the most ethnically diverse states in Nigeria with over 80 distinct ethnic groups. The Mambilla Plateau, located in the state, is the highest plateau in Nigeria and offers a cool, temperate climate.
states.economy: Taraba's economy is agricultural. The Mambilla Plateau supports tea and coffee farming, making the state one of Nigeria's few tea producers. Other crops include yam, cassava, and maize. The state has potential for tourism.
notable_facts: Known as 'Nature's Gift to the Nation'
Home to the Mambilla Plateau, the highest plateau in Nigeria
One of the most ethnically diverse states with over 80 ethnic groups

states.name: Yobe
states.summary: Yobe State is located in northeastern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Damaturu. The state was created in 1991 from part of Borno State. Yobe borders Niger and Chad to the north. The state is predominantly arid and is home to the Kanuri, Fulani, and other ethnic groups. Lake Chad, though now significantly reduced, was historically accessible from this region.
states.economy: Yobe's economy is primarily agricultural and pastoral. Major crops include millet, sorghum, cowpeas, and groundnuts. Livestock rearing is also significant. The state has deposits of diatomite and uranium.
notable_facts: One of Nigeria's most arid states, bordering the Sahel region
Borders two countries: Niger and Chad
Carved out of Borno State in 1991

states.name: Zamfara
states.summary: Zamfara State is located in northwestern Nigeria. Its capital and largest city is Gusau. The state was created in 1996 from part of Sokoto State. Zamfara made national and international headlines in 2000 as the first Nigerian state to implement Sharia law. The state has significant gold deposits and is one of Nigeria's leading gold-producing states.
states.economy: Zamfara's economy is driven by agriculture, gold mining, and animal husbandry. The state is one of Nigeria's largest gold producers. Major crops include millet, sorghum, and groundnuts.
notable_facts: First Nigerian state to implement Sharia law in 2000
One of Nigeria's leading gold-producing states
Carved out of Sokoto State in 1996
